DESCRIPTOR.LABEL: Recommended Metadata for Biological Images
DESCRIPTOR.NAME: rembi
DESCRIPTOR.DESCRIPTION: REcommended Metadata for Biological Images (REMBI) provides a way to explain how images have been generated, providing enough context to allow others to interpret them without reference to external sources.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: study
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Study

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study

FIELD.LABEL: Title
FIELD.NAME: title
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The title for your dataset. This will be displayed when search results including your data are shown. Often this will be the same as an associated publication.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: SARS-COV-2 drug repurposing - Caco2 cell line

FIELD.LABEL: Description
FIELD.NAME: description
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Use this field to describe your dataset. This can be the abstract to an accompanying publication.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: High-throughput screening of repurposed drugs against SARS-CoV-2 in Caco-2 cells

FIELD.LABEL: Release Date
FIELD.NAME: private_until_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The date until which the data remains private and embargoed.

FIELD.LABEL: Keywords
FIELD.NAME: keywords
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Keywords describing your data that can be used to aid search and classification.

FIELD.LABEL: Licence
FIELD.NAME: licence
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The license under which the data are available.

TEXT_VALUE.VALUE: Creative Commons Attribution Share Alike 4.0 International
GNU General Public License v3.0 or later

FIELD.LABEL: Funding Statement
FIELD.NAME: funding_statement
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A description of how the data generation was funded.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Data generation for this study was supported by a grant from the BBSRC, which funded annotation and analysis activities.

FIELD.LABEL: Acknowledgements
FIELD.NAME: acknowledgements
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Any people or groups that should be acknowledged as part of the dataset.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: We acknowledge the contributions of the field research team at the University of Edinburgh, the sequencing support from the Earlham Institute, and funding provided by the BBSRC. Special thanks to local conservation volunteers for assistance in sample collection.

FIELD.LABEL: Rembi Version
FIELD.NAME: rembi_version
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The version of REMBI. The current version to be used is 1.5.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: grant_reference
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Grant Reference

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study

FIELD.LABEL: Identifier
FIELD.NAME: identifier
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The identifier for the grant.

FIELD.LABEL: Funder
FIELD.NAME: funder
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The funding body provididing support.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council (BBSRC)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: publication
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Publication

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study

FIELD.LABEL: Title
FIELD.NAME: title
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Title of associated publication.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: High-throughput drug screening identifies potential SARS-CoV-2 inhibitors in Caco2 cells

FIELD.LABEL: Authors
FIELD.NAME: authors
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Authors of the associated publication. Multiple authors should be listed in order of contribution. Each name should be formatted as Last name, First initial (e.g. Doe, J.). Separate multiple authors with commas.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Doe J., Lee A., Gupta R., Zhao L., Thompson M.

FIELD.LABEL: DOI
FIELD.NAME: doi
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A Digital Object Identifier (DOI) is a unique alphanumeric string assigned to a digital object, such as a journal article, dataset, or publication, to provide a permanent link to its location on the internet. It ensures reliable citation and access. The DOI should follow the standard format (e.g., 10.1234/example.doi) and link to the original source of the publication or data referenced.

FIELD.LABEL: Year
FIELD.NAME: year
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Year of publication.

FIELD.LABEL: Pubmed ID
FIELD.NAME: pubmed_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: PubMed identifier for the publication.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: link
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Link

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study

FIELD.LABEL: Link URL
FIELD.NAME: link_url
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The URL of a link relevant to the dataset.

FIELD.LABEL: Link Type
FIELD.NAME: link_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The type of the link.

FIELD.LABEL: Link Description
FIELD.NAME: link_description
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The description of the linked content.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: study_component
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Study Component

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study

FIELD.LABEL: Study Component ID
FIELD.NAME: study_component_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study component

FIELD.LABEL: Name
FIELD.NAME: name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The name of your study component.

FIELD.LABEL: Description
FIELD.NAME: description
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: An explanation of your study component.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: annotations
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Annotations

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation ID
FIELD.NAME: annotation_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the image annotation record.

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation Overview
FIELD.NAME: annotation_overview
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Short descriptive summary indicating the type of annotation and how it was generated
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Cell nuclei marked using DAPI staining.

FIELD.LABEL: File Type
FIELD.NAME: file_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The format of the annotation file.

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation Type
FIELD.NAME: annotation_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Defines the type of annotation (e.g., class_labels, bounding_boxes, counts, derived_annotations).

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation Method
FIELD.NAME: annotation_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of how the annotations where created. Including protocols used for consensus and quality assurance, if applicable.

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation Criteria
FIELD.NAME: annotation_criteria
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Rules used to generate annotations
FIELD.EXAMPLE: only nuclei in focus were segmented

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation Coverage
FIELD.NAME: annotation_coverage
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The proportion of images from the dataset that were annotated.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: All data that satisfied the Annotation Criteria were annotated.

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation Confidence Level
FIELD.NAME: annotation_confidence_level
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Confidence on annotation accuracy
FIELD.EXAMPLE: more than 95% pixel consensus where multiple annotators independently segmented the same object

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: person
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Person

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study

FIELD.LABEL: Person ID
FIELD.NAME: person_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the author.

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation ID
FIELD.NAME: annotation_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the image annotation record.

FIELD.LABEL: Author First Name
FIELD.NAME: givenName
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A first name (or given name) is the personal name given to an individual conducting the study.

FIELD.LABEL: Author Last Name
FIELD.NAME: familyName
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A last name (or surname) is the family name passed down from one generation to the next for the individual conducting the study.

FIELD.LABEL: Email Address
FIELD.NAME: email
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique identifier used to send and receive electronic messages (emails) over the internet.

FIELD.LABEL: Orcid ID
FIELD.NAME: orcid_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A 16-digit number that uniquely identify researchers.

FIELD.LABEL: Affiliation or Institution
FIELD.NAME: affiliation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A URL to a public registry containing organisation information or the name of the organisation. A Research Organisation Registry (ROR) URL is recommended if a URL is provided.

FIELD.LABEL: Role
FIELD.NAME: role
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Author role in the study. If multiple separate by pipe sybom

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Sample

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study

FIELD.LABEL: Sample ID
FIELD.NAME: sample_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this sample

FIELD.LABEL: Scientific Name or Organism
FIELD.NAME: scientific_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The formal Latin name used to identify the organism from which the sample was derived (e.g. Homo sapiens or Arabidopsis thaliana). This name must accurately correspond to the Taxon ID provided to ensure correct taxonomic classification.

FIELD.LABEL: Taxon ID
FIELD.NAME: taxon_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique identifier (usually from a recognized taxonomy database like NCBI Taxonomy) that corresponds to the organism’s scientific name. It must be accurately matched to the provided scientificName to maintain consistency and traceability in biological records.

FIELD.LABEL: Biosample Accession
FIELD.NAME: biosampleAccession
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique identifier assigned to a biological sample after it has been submitted to a public database, such as the NCBI BioSample or ENA. It serves as a permanent reference to that specific sample, allowing researchers to retrieve metadata and link it across studies or datasets.

FIELD.LABEL: Biological Entity
FIELD.NAME: biological_entity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: What is being imaged

FIELD.LABEL: Common Name
FIELD.NAME: common_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Common name

FIELD.LABEL: Description
FIELD.NAME: description
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: High level description of sample.

FIELD.LABEL: Intrinsic Variables
FIELD.NAME: intrinsic_variables
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Intrinsic (e.g. genetic) alteration if applicable
FIELD.EXAMPLE: stable overexpression of HIST1H2BJ-mCherry and LMNA

FIELD.LABEL: Extrinsic Variables
FIELD.NAME: extrinsic_variables
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: External sample treatment (e.g. reagent) if applicable

FIELD.LABEL: Experimental Variables
FIELD.NAME: experimental_variables
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: What is intentionally varied (e.g. time) between multiple entries in this study component

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: specimen
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Specimen

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study

FIELD.LABEL: Specimen ID
FIELD.NAME: specimen_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this specimen

FIELD.LABEL: Sample ID
FIELD.NAME: sample_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this sample

FIELD.LABEL: Study Component ID
FIELD.NAME: study_component_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study component

FIELD.LABEL: Sample Preparation
FIELD.NAME: sample_preparation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: How the sample was prepared for imaging.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Cells were cultured on poly-L-lysine treated coverslips. Culture media was aspirated, and coverslips were washed once with PBS. Cells were fixed by incubating for 10 min with 4 % formaldehyde/PBS, washed twice with PBS, and permeabilized by incubating (>3 h, -20°C) in 70 % ethanol. Cells were rehydrated by incubating (5 min, RT) with FISH wash buffer (10 % formamide, 2x SSC). For hybridization, coverslips were placed cell-coated side down on a 48μl drop containing 100 nM Quasar570-labelled probes complementary to one of REV-ERBα, CRY2, or TP53 transcripts (Biosearch Technologies) (see Table S6 for probe sequences), 0.1 g/ml dextran sulfate, 1 mg/ml E. coli tRNA, 2 mM VRC, 20 μg/ml BSA, 2x SSC, 10 % formamide and incubated (37°C, 20 h) in a sealed parafilm chamber. Coverslips were twice incubated (37°C, 30 min) in pre-warmed FISH wash buffer, then in PBS containing 0.5 μg/ml 4’,6-diamidino-2-phenylindole (DAPI) (5 min, RT), washed twice with PBS, dipped in water, air-dried, placed cell-coated side down on a drop of ProLong Diamond Antifade Mountant (Life Technologies), allowed to polymerize for 24 h in the dark and then sealed with nail varnish.

FIELD.LABEL: Growth Protocol
FIELD.NAME: growth_protocol
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: How the specimen was grown, e.g. cell line cultures, crosses or plant growth.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Cells grown on coverslips were fixed in ice-cold methanol at _20 _ C for 10 min. After blocking in 0.2% gelatine from cold-water fish (Sigma) in PBS (PBS/FSG) for 15 min, coverslips were incubated with primary antibodies in blocking solution for 1h. Following washes with 0.2% PBS/FSG, the cells were incubated with a 1:500 dilution of secondary antibodies for 1 h (donkey anti- mouse/rabbit/goat/sheep conjugated to Alexa 488 or Alexa 594; Molecular Probes or donkey anti-mouse conjugated to DyLight 405, Jackson ImmunoResearch). The cells were counterstained with 1 _g ml_1 Hoechst 33342 (Sigma) to visualize chromatin. After washing with 0.2% PBS/FSG, the coverslips were mounted on glass slides by inverting them into mounting solution (ProLong Gold antifade, Molecular Probes). The samples were allowed to cure for 24-48 h.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: image_acquisition
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Image Acquisition

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study

FIELD.LABEL: Image Acquisition ID
FIELD.NAME: image_acquisition_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the image acquisition

FIELD.LABEL: Specimen ID
FIELD.NAME: specimen_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this specimen

FIELD.LABEL: Image Method
FIELD.NAME: image_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: What method was used to capture images.

FIELD.LABEL: Imaging Instrument
FIELD.NAME: imaging_instrument
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of the instrument used to capture the images.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: DeltaVision OMX V3 Blaze system (GE Healthcare) equipped with a 60x/1.42 NA PlanApo oil immersion objective (Olympus), pco.edge 5.5 sCMOS cameras (PCO) and 405, 488, 593 and 640 nm lasers

FIELD.LABEL: Image Acquisition Parameters
FIELD.NAME: image_acquisition_parameters
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: How the images were acquired, including instrument settings/parameters.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Embryos were imaged on a Luxendo MuVi SPIM light-sheet microscope, using 30x magnification setting on the Nikon 10x/0.3 water objective. The 488 nm laser was used to image nuclei (His-GFP), and the 561 nm laser was used to image transcriptional dots (MCP-mCherry), both at 5% laser power. Exposure time for the green channel was 55 ms and exposure for the red channel was 70 ms. The line illumination tool was used to improve background levels and was set to 40 pixels.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: image_analysis
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Image Analysis

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study

FIELD.LABEL: Image Analysis ID
FIELD.NAME: image_analysis_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the image analysis

FIELD.LABEL: Study Component ID
FIELD.NAME: study_component_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study component

FIELD.LABEL: Analysis Overview
FIELD.NAME: analysis_overview
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: How image analysis was carried out.
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Each 3D-SIM image contained one nucleus (in a small number of cases multiple nuclei were present, which did not affect the analysis). The image analysis pipeline contained six main steps: bivalent skeleton tracing, trace fluorescence intensity quantification, HEI10 peak detection, HEI10 foci identification, HEI10 foci intensity quantification, and total bivalent intensity quantification. Note that the normalization steps used for foci identification differ from those used for foci intensity quantification; the former was intended to robustly identify foci from noisy traces, whilst the latter was used to carefully quantify foci HEI10 levels.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: image_correlation
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: Image Correlation

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study

FIELD.LABEL: Image Correlation ID
FIELD.NAME: image_correlation_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the image correlation

FIELD.LABEL: Image Analysis ID
FIELD.NAME: image_analysis_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the image analysis

FIELD.LABEL: Spatial and Temporal Alignment
FIELD.NAME: spatial_and_temporal_alignment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Method used to correlate images from different modalities (e.g. manual overlay, alignment algorithm etc)

FIELD.LABEL: Fiducials Used
FIELD.NAME: fiducials_used
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Features from correlated datasets used for colocalisation

FIELD.LABEL: Transformation Matrix or Other Information
FIELD.NAME: transformation_matrix
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Correlation transformations
FIELD.EXAMPLE: Translation and rotation matrix applied using ImageJ plugin

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: file_level_metadata
FIELD_GROUP.LABEL: File Level Metadata

FIELD.LABEL: Study ID
FIELD.NAME: study_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study

FIELD.LABEL: File ID
FIELD.NAME: file_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this file

FIELD.LABEL: Study Component ID
FIELD.NAME: study_component_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study component

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation ID
FIELD.NAME: annotation_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the image annotation record.

FIELD.LABEL: Image File name
FIELD.NAME: source_image_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The file name of the image including the extension. Common file names end with tiff, jpeg, png, gif, bmp, and ome-tiff etc.

FIELD.LABEL: Transformations
FIELD.NAME: transformations
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Any preprocessing or transformations applied to the image.

FIELD.LABEL: Spatial Information
FIELD.NAME: spatial_information
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Spatial resolution, scale, or coordinate info related to the image.

FIELD.LABEL: Annotation Creation Time
FIELD.NAME: annotation_creation_time
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Timestamp of when the annotation was created.
